content: Twisting atomically thin magnetic layers does more than reshape their electronics it can create giant, topological magnetic textures. In chromium triiodide, researchers observed skyrmion-like patterns stretching far beyond the expected moir  scale, reaching hundreds of nanometers. Even more surprising, their size doesn t simply follow the twist pattern but peaks at a specific angle. This twist-controlled magnetism could pave the way for low-power spintronic devices built from geometry alone.

metadata.summary: Twisting atomically thin magnetic layers does more than reshape their electronics it can create giant, topological magnetic textures
